message:
feat(stdio): add fgetln + fpurge (BSD line reader + purge) (bd-lwns7)

BSD fgetln(stream, *len) reads a logical line from `stream` (up
to and including the trailing newline) and returns a pointer into
a thread-local buffer plus the line length via *len. Used by
tail(1), grep(1), sort(1) and many BSD ports.

BSD fpurge(stream) is a thin wrapper over our existing __fpurge
that returns int (the BSD signature) instead of void. Currently
both are missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi.

* stdio_abi::fgetln uses a thread_local! Vec<u8> for the buffer
  so the returned pointer remains valid until the next fgetln on
  the same thread (matches BSD's documented contract). Reads via
  fgetc one byte at a time. Returns NULL on EOF (with *len = 0)
  AND when the only data is a partial last line, returns it
  without a trailing newline.
* stdio_abi::fpurge delegates to __fpurge and translates the GNU
  void return into the BSD int 0/-1. NULL stream yields -1.

8 abi integration tests (6 fgetln, 2 fpurge): line-with-newline,
two consecutive lines, last-line-without-newline + subsequent
NULL on EOF, immediate EOF on empty file, NULL stream guard, NULL
len pointer safety; fpurge zero-return on valid stream and -1 on
NULL stream. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
